description_long: This house features a large, steeply pitched thatched roof with extended eaves, supported by wooden posts that elevate the structure above the ground. The front facade displays a prominent circular wooden ornament above a central entryway, flanked by two small enclosed porches with traditional sliding doors and decorative banners. The exterior walls are primarily constructed of wood and brick, with visible timber framing and a raised wooden platform serving as a veranda. The side and rear views reveal a consistent use of thatch roofing and wooden siding, with minimal window openings and a simple, functional layout. The roof includes a decorative ridge beam and a smaller gabled extension on one side, adding architectural interest. The overall design evokes traditional Japanese minka or rural farmhouse architecture, emphasizing natural materials and elevated construction.
description: A traditional Japanese-style house with a steep thatched roof, wooden posts, and a raised platform, featuring a central entryway and minimal windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway, circular wooden ornament, two small porches with sliding doors, and decorative banners.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long thatched roof, raised platform, and a small gabled extension; minimal window openings are visible.
description_view_2: Rear view with a simpler facade, a smaller circular ornament, and consistent thatched roofing; no visible entryway.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the extended thatched roof, raised platform, and two large sliding windows.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with large sliding windows and minimal openings
attribute_entryway_design: Central entryway with a wooden door, flanked by small porches with sliding doors, decorative banners, and a prominent circular ornament above; raised platform with steps and overhanging roof.
